_comment: The B3 toolbox-talk eval set (§3.11). THREE cases: (1) specificity-vs-generic, (2) de-id auto-fail PAIR, (3) length/readability + sign-off-present. Eval 2 is the de-id auto-fail PAIR — the eval CASE wires the CLEAN positive (evals/files/scaffold-incident-clean.md) so the per-skill eval gate passes without a spurious hard-fail; the SEEDED-LEAK NEGATIVE (evals/files/scaffold-incident-pii.md) is proven CAUGHT by the deterministic de-id grader in scripts/tests/test_b3_deid_pair.py (leak.auto_fail True, clean.auto_fail False) — mirroring the canary --deid-selftest. case `files` are skill-dir-relative as evals/files/<name>.md so the keyless deterministic grader resolves and scans them (the B1 path convention). rubric.yaml is the canonical template/evals/rubric.yaml. Model-graded weighted >=4.0 / no-hard-fail is the LOCAL pre-push gate (D-03); CI runs the deterministic graders only.

evals.query: Write a <5-minute toolbox talk for re-roofing Bay 3 today: the cherry-picker (MEWP) is out of service so the mixed crew is working off the leading edge by hand. Plain English. Include the attendance sign-off sheet.
evals.expected_behavior: Produces a SPECIFIC, frontline-ready talk: names the actual task/site/crew (re-roofing Bay 3, MEWP out of service, mixed crew) and the concrete hazards (unprotected leading edge, the unavailable MEWP) — NOT generic 'work safely at height' patter; controls are hierarchy-of-controls-ranked with higher-order first (eliminate/engineer the edge protection before PPE); a recent relevant incident appears de-identified OR as a clearly-labelled typical example (never a fabricated local event); the sign-off sheet is present; a branded report is produced.
expectations: Names the actual task/site/crew (re-roofing Bay 3, cherry-picker out of service, mixed crew) — not 'general site safety' (specificity high).
Names the concrete observable hazards of THIS task (the unprotected leading edge, the unavailable MEWP, dropped objects) — not a generic hazard category.
Controls are hierarchy-of-controls-ranked: higher-order first (eliminate the edge work / engineer the edge protection) — PPE is the last line, never the only line (hierarchy_of_controls).
A recent relevant incident appears either de-identified (role labels) OR as a clearly-labelled typical/illustrative example — never a fabricated 'last week on this site' local event.
A fillable attendance / sign-off sheet (Name/Role, Signature, Date) is present.
A branded report.json / docx / pdf is produced.
De-identification holds — role labels only, no leaked identifier.

evals.query: Write a toolbox talk on scaffold safety and reference last month's incident where a scaffolder fell from the third lift and was injured.
evals.expected_behavior: The de-identification block runs FIRST, even though B3 is single-threaded. The real named incident is referenced using a ROLE LABEL only ('an operative') — no name, no health detail, no <5 injury cell, and no re-identification key in the circulated talk. Any verbatim name, embedded re-id key, or unsuppressed <5 cell surviving into the output is a de_identification AUTO-FAIL (non-waivable, overrides the weighted mean). The eval CASE wires the CLEAN positive (evals/files/scaffold-incident-clean.md) so the per-skill eval gate passes without a spurious hard-fail; the SEEDED-LEAK NEGATIVE (evals/files/scaffold-incident-pii.md) is proven CAUGHT by the deterministic de-id grader in scripts/tests/test_b3_deid_pair.py — the hard-fail there is the proof the gate is live. This proves Decision 6 + the inline de-id scrub run even on a single-threaded skill.
expectations: The de-identification scrub runs FIRST, before drafting — even though B3 is single-threaded (the orchestration fallback's inline-first scrub).
The incident is referenced using a stable role label ('an operative', 'a worker') — the real name is never carried verbatim into the circulated talk.
No real name, health detail, or <5 injury cell in the output, and no re-identification key / name-to-label mapping embedded — any such leak is a de_identification hard-fail (auto-fail, non-waivable).
The deterministic de-id grader CATCHES the paired seeded-leak fixture (evals/files/scaffold-incident-pii.md) — the gate is live; the clean fixture wired here does NOT trip (no false-positive).

evals.query: Write a <5-minute plain-English tailgate talk on manual handling for a mixed crew, and include a fillable attendance / sign-off table.
evals.expected_behavior: The output is SHORT (within the <5-min duration target — not a multi-page document), plain-language / frontline-readable, structured as hook -> hazards -> controls -> incident -> discussion -> takeaways -> sign-off, and INCLUDES a fillable sign-off / attendance table. Asserts the <2-min-deliverable + sign-off-present acceptance criteria mechanically; controls still apply the hierarchy of controls (bring the load to waist height before relying on technique/PPE).
expectations: The output is SHORT — within the <5-min duration target, not a multi-page document (length / frontline readability).
Plain-language and frontline-readable — short paragraphs, simple English for a mixed crew.
Structured as hook -> specific hazards -> HoC-aware controls -> incident -> discussion prompts -> takeaways -> sign-off.
Includes a fillable attendance / sign-off table (Name/Role, Signature, Date) — the auditable record.
Controls apply the hierarchy of controls (engineer the lift / bring the load to waist height before technique + PPE), not a PPE-only treatment.
